description:
You are an edge–computing engineer preparing an IoT rollout.  
Inside the workspace the following configuration file is already present:

• /home/user/deployment/edge_device_config.json

Your objective is to perform a *one–liner* JSON-schema-style check with `jq` and to capture a compact, human-readable report.

Validation rules (they are intentionally simple and must all hold for an object to be considered valid):
1. The JSON file is a top-level array whose elements are individual device objects.
2. Every device object must have **all three** of the following keys:  
   • id  
   • location  
   • status  

Expected report format (this is what the automated grader will parse; keep it exact, including capitalisation, the equal signs, and the terminating newline at the end of the file):

TOTAL_DEVICES=<integer>
VALID_OBJECTS=<integer>
INVALID_OBJECTS=<integer>

Where  
• <integer> on line 1 is the total number of elements in the array,  
• <integer> on line 2 is the count of objects that satisfy **all** validation rules,  
• <integer> on line 3 is the count of objects that fail **any** rule.

Task:
1. Using a single terminal command that relies on `jq`, generate the above three-line summary.  
2. Redirect the command’s standard output to the file

/home/user/deployment/validation_report.txt

The file must contain nothing except the three lines described and finish with a newline character.

Notes & constraints:
• Do not move or rename any supplied files.  
• Do not manually edit /home/user/deployment/edge_device_config.json.  
• The entire job (validation + redirecting the output) must be executed by *one* shell command.  
• No extra explanatory text may be printed to the report file—only the three required lines.  
• If you need to inspect intermediate results, feel free to issue other diagnostic commands, but the graded deliverable is the final content of validation_report.txt.

When finished, you can cat the report to the terminal so a human operator sees the outcome, but the automated test will examine the file on disk.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state:

1. Directory  
   /home/user/deployment  
   ‑ created and writable (permissions 0755).

2. File  
   /home/user/deployment/edge_device_config.json  
   with exactly these bytes (no trailing spaces, Unix newline endings):

[
  {"id":"dev-001","location":"NYC","status":"active"},
  {"id":"dev-002","location":"Berlin","status":"inactive"},
  {"id":"dev-003","location":"London","status":"active"},
  {"location":"Tokyo","status":"inactive"},
  {"id":"dev-005","location":"Paris","status":"inactive"}
]

3. Desired artefact after the agent completes the task:

File  
/home/user/deployment/validation_report.txt  
containing exactly (including the final newline):

TOTAL_DEVICES=5
VALID_OBJECTS=4
INVALID_OBJECTS=1

Permissions on the new file should be 0644 or more permissive so the grader can read it.
